name: Pu'u o Mahuka Heiau
tagline: Oahu's largest ancient Hawaiian temple — a sacred 17th-century site on a bluff overlooking Waimea Bay with breathtaking views.
parking: Small gravel lot at the end of Pupukea Rd
bestTime: Sunrise or sunset for the most powerful atmosphere
talkStory: Oahu's largest heiau spans nearly two acres on a bluff 250 feet above Waimea Bay and was a luakini heiau — a site of human sacrifice reserved for the highest-ranking chiefs. In 1794, three of Captain George Vancouver's crew members from the Daedalus were reportedly sacrificed here. The kahuna nui (high priest) used this site to communicate with the gods and predict the future. Today, you'll see fresh ti-leaf-wrapped stone offerings left by practitioners — never disturb these. The panoramic views of Waimea Valley, the bay, and the winter surf are reason enough to make the drive up Pupukea Road.
